mission.title: opentelemetry-operator: Autoscaler doesn't scale deployment when MinReplicas is set
mission.description: Autoscaler doesn't scale deployment when MinReplicas is set. This issue affects 16+ users.

steps.title: Identify opentelemetry-operator troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your opentelemetry-operator deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator -n opentelemetry-operator --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-operator configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-operator configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-operator
```
When using HPA with OpenTelemetryCollector, the deployment doesn't scale when HPA decides it needs more replicas.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Autoscaler doesn't scale deployment when MinReplicas is set
steps.description:
<Describe what has changed.>
The issue was already well described in the original report. However, the operator currently always returns the values defined in spec, and autoscaler.minValues never scales up. I’ve fixed this problem. I also renamed the function to GetDesiredReplicas, since it’s not
```yaml
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1beta1
   kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
   metadata:
     name: backend-traces
     namespace: monitoring
   spec:
     autoscaler:
       minReplicas: 3
       maxReplicas: 6
       targetCPUUtilization: 80
       targetMemoryUtilization: 50
     # ... rest of collector config
```

steps.title: Confirm Autoscaler doesn't scale deployment when… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator -n opentelemetry-operator --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
<Describe what has changed.>
The issue was already well described in the original report. However, the operator currently always returns the values defined in spec, and autoscaler.minValues never scales up. I’ve fixed this problem. I also renamed the function to GetDesiredReplicas, since it’s not only called during initialization but also on every reconciliation.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1beta1
   kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
   metadata:
     name: backend-traces
     namespace: monitoring
   spec:
     autoscaler:
       minReplicas: 3
       maxReplicas: 6
       targetCPUUtilization: 80
       targetMemoryUtilization: 50
     # ... rest of collector config

❯ k get hpa backend-traces-collector
    NAME                       REFERENCE                               TARGETS            MINPODS   MAXPODS   REPLICAS   AGE
    backend-traces-collector   OpenTelemetryCollector/backend-traces   62%/50%, 42%/80%   3         6        5           386d

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-operator installed or the issue environment reproducible.
